checklist.claim: CLAUDE.md gains the wrapped-literal gotcha (tr -s, rendered surface)
13 agent-mode items dispatched to devflow:checklist-verifier; results not collected before loop wrap-up (verifier agents still running at coordinator-directed completion)
checklist.evidence: dispatched 13 verifiers; orchestrator-direct verification substituted for the two REJECT-driving claims (help= conditionality: confirmed then FIXED in 53a0557; CI-runs-as-root comment: confirmed false then FIXED in 53a0557)

phase3_findings.description: multi-literal help= FAIL is conditional on the phrase being off-line while the AC and four doc sites state it unconditionally (reproduced on fixture)
diff-added comment at run.sh:20253 claims 'CI runs as root' — false against the shipped ubuntu-latest non-container CI (documented_falsehood, self-contradicting-diff carve-out)
engine skill bodies hardcode assert_pin_red_under (DevFlow-suite-only), making the behavioral-fix-pin guard inert in consumer repos
kind=='none' resolved-but-unlintable targets silently exempt from the comment lint (latent; no current corpus member)
real-corpus scan surfaces only UNRESOLVED-COUNT (unbounded), discards per-site detail
synthetic self-test invocations drop scanner exit status; wrapped call discards stderr
hash_comment_regions applies shell comment rules to .py targets (two narrow fail-open edges)

fix_decisions.claim_text: multi-literal help= FAIL conditional vs unconditional docs/AC
fix_decisions.evidence: code brought to spec (issue #375 AC mandates unconditional); mutation-verified: old scanner missed helponline fixture, new scanner flags it; real corpus stays clean (suite 4658/0)

fix_decisions.claim_text: diff-added comment 'CI runs as root' false against HEAD
fix_decisions.evidence: reworded to conditional root-run-suite form; ci.yml runs-on ubuntu-latest with no container (non-root runner) is the shipped truth

fix_decisions.claim_text: skill bodies should stay framework-agnostic; assert_pin_red_under naming belongs only in the prompt extension
fix_decisions.evidence: issue #375 AC mandates verbatim that phase-2-implement.md section 2.3 and review-and-fix SKILL.md Step 3 item 4 are rewritten to route through assert_pin_red_under at those exact sites; the prescription contradicts the spec-of-record. CLAUDE.md scopes the repo-agnostic constraint to the two vendored superpowers skills (receiving-code-review, requesting-code-review) only. Making the engine text generic again would un-implement the AC; a consumer-facing generalization is follow-up-issue material, not a defect in this PR.

fix_decisions.claim_text: kind=='none' resolved-but-unlintable targets silently exempt from comment lint
fix_decisions.evidence: latent by construction: every current corpus target has a covered extension or an --md binding (matches the standalone cloud review's Minor 7, already parked in the approve-with-notes report); revisit when a pin targets an uncovered extension
sentinel guards added for PRU_FX/PRU_META (corroborated by 2 agents; trivial, clearly correct, adjacent to the over-broad fix block)
the code comment deliberately chose surface-not-assert; an upper bound is hardening for a follow-up, not a defect (2 agents agree it is not a contract violation)
fail-closed already (positive assertions go RED on empty output per the finding's own analysis); diagnosability polish only
narrow edges; no current corpus member exercises them; tokenize-based pass is follow-up material
grade kept: a consumer-repo directive pointing at an absent helper is a real fail-open in deployed contexts; disposition is pushback on spec grounds (the AC mandates this design), not severity

notes: Run executed by the fix subagent for PR #391 inside /devflow:review-and-fix invoked from the shepherd fix procedure. Phase 3 dispatched 6 agents (5 returned results consumed: checklist-generator, silent-failure-hunter, requesting-code-review final pass, plus orchestrator-direct 4.1.6 truthfulness sweep and 3.1.5 completeness-critic [no finding]; code-reviewer/comment-analyzer/pr-test-analyzer and 13 checklist verifiers had not returned by coordinator-directed wrap-up and are recorded as dispatched-but-uncollected). Phase 2: 2 lite PASS; the two REJECT-driving claims were orchestrator-verified directly and fixed. Dirty-tree backstop: the orchestrator's own Step 3 edits overlapped the dispatch window, so the snapshot compare cannot attribute divergence; agents are read-only-instructed and no unexpected paths were dirty at commit time (git status showed only the two fix files).
